folio.label: 101r
folio.l: Hire brother whicú , that Seuene ©er was of age
Arayed eke ful fressú , in his manere
And thus in gret noblesse , and witú glad chere
Toward Saluces , shapyngö here Iourney
Fro day to day , they ryden in here wey
¢ A mongö al this , after his wykked vsage
This Markys , ©et his wyf to tempte more
To the outrest preue , of hire corage
Fully , to haue , ex¹ience and lore
If that she were , as stedefast as byfore
He on a day , in open audience
Ful boistously , hatú seid hire , this sentence
¢ Certes Grisilde , I had I now plesaunce
To haue ©ow to my wyf , for ©oure goodnesse
As for ©oure trouthe , and for ©oure obeysaunce
Nougút for ©oure lynage , ne for ©oure richesse
But now knowe I , in verray sotúfastnesse
That in gret lordship~ , if I me wel auyse
There is gret seruitute , in sondry wyse
¢ I may nat do , as euäy plowman may
My peeple constreynetú me , to take
A nother wyf , and crien day be day
And eke the Pope , rancour , for to slake
Consentetú it , that dar I vndertake
And trewely , thus meche , I wyl ©ow sey
My newe wyf , is comyngö by the wey
¢ Be strongö of hert , and voide a noon hire place
And thilke dower , that ©e brougúten me
Take it a geyn , I gunte it of my grace
Returnetú to ©oure fadres hous , q¶ he
Noman may alwey , haue ¸s¹ite
Witú euen hert , I rede ©ow to endure
The stroke of fortune , or of auenture
¢ And she a geyn answered , in pacience
My lord q¶ she , I wot and wyst alwey
How that bytwene , ©oure magnificence
And my pouäte , no wygút ne can ne may
Maken com¹isou~ , it is no nay
I ne held me neuäe digne , in no manere
To be ©oure wyf , ne ©oure chaumbrere
¢ And in this hous , there ©e me lady made
The heye god take I , for my wytnesse
And also wysly , he my soule glade
I neuäe held me lady , ne maistresse
But humble säuaunt , to ©oure worthynesse
And euäe shal , while æ my lyf may dure
